executive_summary: Strong signal week with high-urgency picks across Markets and Payments. Your NIFTY CE/PE strategy gets serious infrastructure: NautilusTrader and Lean offer production-grade backtesting, while QuestDB solves tick data storage at the latency you need. On payments, Hyperswitch is the most relevant orchestration layer I've surfaced for Redpin—worth a proper technical evaluation. The planning-with-files tool addresses your n8n workflow fragility directly.

top_picks.headline: Rust-speed backtester for your NIFTY options strategy
top_picks.why_now: Your first-5-minute candle CE/PE strategy needs deterministic backtesting before going live; NautilusTrader's event-driven engine handles options with the precision you need.
top_picks.action: Clone repo, run the options example notebook with 3 months of NIFTY data to validate strategy edge before next expiry cycle.

top_picks.headline: Open-source payments orchestration for Redpin's multi-processor stack
top_picks.why_now: You're managing JP Morgan Chase + other processors manually; Hyperswitch handles routing, failover, and reconciliation at scale—PCI-compliant out of box.
top_picks.action: Schedule 2-hour technical spike: deploy local instance, test mock transactions through your current processor endpoints, document integration gaps.

top_picks.headline: Sub-millisecond tick storage for options analysis
top_picks.why_now: Your backtesting accuracy depends on clean tick data; QuestDB's time-series performance beats Postgres by 10x for this workload.
top_picks.action: Spin up QuestDB container, ingest 1 week of NIFTY options ticks via yfinance, benchmark query latency against your current setup.

top_picks.headline: Crash-proof state for your n8n AI workflows
top_picks.why_now: Your automation workflows lose context on interruption; this markdown-based persistence pattern is lightweight and immediately applicable.
top_picks.action: Implement planning-with-files pattern in one existing n8n agentic workflow, test recovery after forced interruption.

top_picks.headline: Quick options data pipeline for backtesting
top_picks.why_now: You need historical NIFTY options chains before NautilusTrader backtesting; yfinance is the fastest path to clean data.
top_picks.action: Write Python script to pull 6 months NIFTY options chains, validate data completeness for your strike range.

skip_list.reason: NautilusTrader covers same use case with better Rust performance for your latency requirements.
Reference list, not actionable—you already have specific tools identified.
Overlaps with yfinance + NautilusTrader stack; adds complexity without unique value this week.

> **Maintenance notice — June 2026**
> Due to funding constraints, the public [Fincept Terminal](https://github.com/Fincept-Corporation/FinceptTerminal) is moving to **one update per month** and is **no longer maintained on a daily basis**. The team is now focused on a subscription-based **private edition** of the terminal and a new project, **[Quantcept](https://github.com/Fincept-Corporation/Quantcept)**. This open-source repo stays public and will not be deleted.
